label: vulnerable

source.description: User-controlled string from query parameter 'title' received in the route layer. No character filter or length limit is applied.

propagation.description: title is forwarded unchanged to ReportService.render_preview() — no validation at the route-to-service boundary.
ReportService.render_preview() passes title directly to render_report_template() without any sanitization — no validation at the service-to-utils boundary.
title is concatenated with the '+' operator directly into the HTML template source string, embedding user input as raw Jinja2 template syntax.

sink.description: Environment.from_string() parses and compiles the attacker-controlled string as a Jinja2 template. Any Jinja2 expressions embedded in title (e.g. {{config}}) are evaluated on render().

missing_controls: use a static template with {{ title }} placeholder and pass title as a context variable to render() — never concatenate user input into the template source string
allowlist / regex filter stripping Jinja2 control characters ({{ }} % #) before concatenation as defense-in-depth
expected_findings.risk_reasoning: The title value propagates through three layers (route → service → utils) without any validation. It is concatenated into the Jinja2 template source string in the utils layer. An attacker supplying '{{config}}' or '{{''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__()}}' can evaluate arbitrary expressions.
expected_findings.remediation_hint:
Define a static template '

{{ title }}

Report Preview

' and pass title as a context variable: _env.from_string(STATIC_TEMPLATE).render(title=title). Never concatenate user input into the template source string.

recall_anchor.description: A correct system must identify the sink in utils/template_utils.py where user input is concatenated into the template source and passed to from_string(). Source identification in routes/report_routes.py is additional credit.

notes: This case tests cross-file SSTI taint tracing: route layer (source) → service layer (passthrough) → utils layer (concat into template source + from_string sink). The wrapping in HTML tags makes the concat less obvious than the FILE-mode case.
